name: Ratatoskr
description: Ratatoskr is a squirrel in Norse mythology who runs up and down the world tree Yggdrasil to carry messages between the eagle perched at the top and the serpent Níðhöggr, who gnaws at the roots. He is primarily depicted as a mischievous and gossipy creature, actively stirring up animosity between the eagle and the serpent by exaggerating and distorting the messages he carries. This constant back-and-forth fuels the eternal conflict at the heart of the Norse cosmos. While not a powerful deity in the traditional sense, Ratatoskr plays a crucial role in maintaining the dynamic, often chaotic, balance of the world tree. He embodies the disruptive forces of rumor and discord, demonstrating how even seemingly small actions can have significant consequences within a larger system. His existence highlights the interconnectedness of the realms and the constant tension between opposing forces. Ratatoskr's actions, while seemingly malicious, contribute to the overall narrative of the Norse cosmos, emphasizing the cyclical nature of creation and destruction. He represents the ever-present potential for conflict and the importance of maintaining vigilance against the spread of misinformation and malicious gossip. He is not worshipped, but his presence within Yggdrasil is a constant reminder of the internal strife that can plague even the most stable structures.
subtitle: The Messenger Squirrel of Yggdrasil

keyMyths.title: The Messenger's Treachery
keyMyths.description: Ratatoskr carries messages between the eagle and Níðhöggr, but twists the words to incite hatred. This highlights his mischievous nature and role in maintaining conflict.

keyMyths.title: Life on Yggdrasil
keyMyths.description: Ratatoskr lives on Yggdrasil, constantly running up and down the trunk. This emphasizes his constant activity and integral part of the world tree's ecosystem.

keyMyths.title: Fueling the Conflict
keyMyths.description: By exaggerating messages, Ratatoskr ensures the eternal conflict between the serpent and the eagle continues. This demonstrates his role in the cosmic balance.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
